idea_summary: Initial tiled matmul: BLOCK_M=128, BLOCK_N=256, BLOCK_K=64, num_stages=3, num_wa
Reduced BLOCK_N from 256 to 128 - performance degraded
Increase num_stages from 3 to 4 - slight regression
num_warps=4 severely degrades performance - insufficient parallelism
Persistent kernel with grid-stride loop - worse for large grids
BLOCK_K=32 with 4 stages - slightly worse than 64x3
BLOCK_K=32 with 6 stages - too many stages hurts
BLOCK_N=192 - Triton requires power-of-2 tile sizes
Eviction hints A=evict_last B=evict_first - slight regression
allow_tf32=True - no improvement for FP16 inputs
Split-K=2 with atomic_add - overhead too high
128x128x64 with 8 warps - less compute per load
